name: Muslim ibn Aqeel
title: The Ambassador of Karbala
shortDescription: The brave and lonely ambassador of Imam Hussain in Kufa, martyred before Ashura due to the betrayal of the Kufans.
fullStory:
Muslim ibn Aqeel is the nephew of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) and the cousin of Imam Hussain (AS). He was one of the brave warriors of Bani Hashem, having commanded the right wing of the Imam's army during the Battle of Siffin. His loyalty, bravery, and wisdom led Imam Hussain (AS) to choose him as his special ambassador and representative in the most sensitive historical circumstances.

When countless letters from the people of Kufa inviting Imam Hussain (AS) reached him, the Imam sent Muslim ibn Aqeel to assess the situation and verify the Kufans' claims. Initially, the reception by the Kufans was unprecedented, and over 18,000 people pledged allegiance to him on behalf of the Imam. Confident of the people's support, Muslim wrote a letter to the Imam urging him to come to Kufa.

However, with the arrival of Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad in Kufa and the establishment of a terrifying martial law, the situation changed rapidly. Ibn Ziyad, by bribing tribal leaders and threatening the populace, created such fear and terror that the Kufans quickly broke their pledges. Muslim, who had once prayed with thousands in the Kufa mosque, found himself completely alone in the dark alleys of Kufa after the Maghrib prayer. No one dared to give him shelter, except for a brave old woman named Taw'a.

According to Sheikh Mufid's report in Al-Irshad, Ibn Ziyad's spies discovered Muslim's hiding place and sent a massive force to arrest him. Muslim fought them single-handedly with exemplary bravery, driving them out of the house multiple times. The enemies, unable to match his sword, threw stones and burning reeds at him from the rooftops. Finally, through deceit and a spear thrust from behind, they wounded and captured him.

When Muslim was brought to the governor's palace before Ibn Ziyad, he spoke with firmness and without fear, complaining about the Kufans' disloyalty. His last request before martyrdom was to send a message to Imam Hussain (AS) to deter him from coming to Kufa. Finally, by Ibn Ziyad's order, Bukayr ibn Humran beheaded him on the roof of the palace and threw his pure body down, making him the first martyr on the path of Karbala.
quoteArabic: ارْجِعْ بِأَهْلِ بَیْتِکَ وَ لا یَغُرَّنَّکَ أَهْلُ الْکُوفَةِ
quote: (In his last message:) Return with your family and do not be deceived by the people of Kufa.
